hyperedges.label: Spec-Kit Design Artifact Flow (spec -> research -> plan -> data-model/contracts -> quickstart -> tasks)
Ephemeral Redis Session Pattern (keys + atomic TTL + SINTER + push expiry)

nodes.label: Redis Public TCP Proxy Requirement
nodes.rationale: Backend must use Railway's public TCP proxy for Redis instead of the internal URL because internal Railway URLs fail during monorepo workspace-isolated builds.

nodes.label: RAILPACK_SPA_OUTPUT_DIR Setting
nodes.rationale: Mandatory for Nixpacks to detect the static SPA in a monorepo; without it Railway fails with 'No start command was found'.

nodes.label: Lua Script Copy Build Step
nodes.rationale: The build command must copy backend/src/redis/*.lua into dist/redis or the backend crashes on startup with 'refresh-ttl.lua not found'.

nodes.label: Set-Intersection Consensus (SINTER)
nodes.rationale: Each participant's likes live in a Redis set so the group's matches are a single SINTER call when the last person submits - no application-level intersection logic.

nodes.label: Push Expiry via Keyspace Notifications
nodes.rationale: Redis keyspace notifications fire on key expiry and the backend broadcasts session:expired, so clients are not left polling a dead session.

nodes.label: Kinetic Warmth Design Philosophy
nodes.rationale: Browse first, contextual actions, minimal friction (no auth for core flow), progressive disclosure: Explore -> Filter -> Detail -> Session.

nodes.label: Page Object Model Test Architecture
nodes.rationale: Each page gets a dedicated class encapsulating locators, actions, and assertions; custom fixtures add multi-participant session setup and WebSocket helpers.

nodes.label: Selection Entity
nodes.rationale: Selections stay private until all participants submit (FR-008, FR-023) and lock immediately after submit (FR-026).

nodes.label: Result Entity (Overlap)
nodes.rationale: Only the intersection of all participants' choices is revealed (FR-010); no automated fallback when empty (FR-016); single participant sees own selections (FR-021).

nodes.label: Honor System (No Auth)
nodes.rationale: No technical prevention of duplicate joins or impersonation (FR-024) - simplest approach that fits the no-accounts philosophy; chosen over device tracking in clarifications.

nodes.label: Redis Key Schema for Sessions
nodes.rationale: All session keys (hash, participants set, per-participant selection sets, results set) share one EXPIREAT timestamp for atomic expiration; ~1.5 KB per session.

nodes.label: Atomic TTL Refresh Lua Script
nodes.rationale: Lua script EXPIREATs every session key with the same absolute timestamp on each activity, preventing staggered expiration; chosen over relative EXPIRE and cleanup jobs.

nodes.label: SINTER Overlap Calculation
nodes.rationale: Set-based selection storage enables O(N*M) Redis SINTER intersection across per-participant selection sets; chosen over a single JSON hash which would prevent SINTER.

nodes.label: Socket.IO Room-Based Architecture Decision
nodes.rationale: 1 session = 1 Socket.IO room gives native scoping, automatic cleanup, participant limit enforcement, and server-side selective broadcasting for privacy (FR-023); namespaces and app-level filtering rejected.

nodes.label: Connection State Recovery Decision
nodes.rationale: Socket.IO 4.6+ replays missed events during disconnects up to 2 minutes, handling mobile network interruptions without custom reconnect logic.

nodes.label: Zustand State Management Decision
nodes.rationale: Zustand (<1KB gzipped) with selective subscriptions prevents re-renders on WebSocket updates; Redux (~15KB) and React Context rejected as too heavy or over-rendering.

nodes.label: Contract-First API Testing Decision
nodes.rationale: OpenAPI for REST plus Zod-validated TypeScript discriminated unions for WebSocket, tested with Vitest and vitest-openapi; monorepo shared/ package makes breaking changes fail TypeScript checks.

nodes.label: WCAG 2.2 AA Mobile Accessibility Decision
nodes.rationale: 44x44px touch targets (beyond WCAG's 24px minimum, avoiding rage taps), 4.5:1 contrast, semantic HTML for VoiceOver/TalkBack; AAA rejected as impractical on small screens.

nodes.label: Uber Eats and DoorDash Deep Links
nodes.norm_label: uber eats and doordash deep links
